production.name: Kitchen Sink
production.description: Snap a photo of your fridge and get instant recipe ideas. AI identifies your ingredients, rates your fridge out of 10, roasts your food choices, and suggests what to cook.
highlights: Claude vision API identifies ingredients from a single fridge photo
AI rates your fridge 1 to 10 on variety, nutrition, and cooking potential
Roast Mode for a savage (but playful) critique of your fridge contents
Editable ingredient list with on-the-fly recipe regeneration
Server-side API proxy keeps keys secure with client-side image compression

production.name: Sup
production.description: "Who's free to hang" PWA. One-tap broadcasting to your squad with push notifications, real-time map with midpoint calculation, and bar suggestions near the group.
highlights: Live updates via WebSockets with automatic polling fallback for reliability
Geographic database queries to calculate distances and parse location data
Calculates the geographic center point between all active users
Server-side push notifications with encrypted signing keys
Database-level access control, so users only see their own squad's data

production.name: MJ Growth Consulting
production.description: Marketing website for a Fortune 100 sales executive's growth advisory practice. Clean, minimal design with image carousel and contact form.
highlights: Swipe-enabled image carousel with touch gesture detection
Scroll-triggered fade-in animations using Intersection Observer
Dynamic schedule that auto-labels the next upcoming event
Contact form with real-time validation and API submission
Performance-optimized font loading that doesn't block page render

production.name: Horizon Alliance
production.description: Alliance hub for Last War: Survival Game. Features event schedules, battle strategies, leadership roster, and alliance lore with a tactical military aesthetic.
highlights: Live countdown timer that converts between user's timezone and game time
Structured data model for multi-phase battle plans with role assignments
Animated mobile menu with 3D transform transitions
Collapsible FAQ and lore sections with custom expand/collapse styling
Pulsing glow animations on event banners to draw attention

experimental.name: Research Intelligence Engine
experimental.description: A self-built agentic research stack that reads financial disclosure at scale, along with the chatter around it. It pulls transcripts (including YouTube), SEC filings, fundamentals, and retail sentiment from Seeking Alpha, Reddit, and X, then flags earnings-theme pulses, 52-week deep dives, and inflection setups early. (Proprietary picks redacted.)

images.caption: System architecture: social and disclosure sources feed four analysis lanes and their outputs (signals redacted)

highlights: Transcript Radar: NLP that compares language across thousands of earnings calls (including transcripts sourced from YouTube) to catch narrative shifts before the market does
Earnings Pulse: a weekly digest that pulls together the main themes from the week's earnings calls
52-week deep dives and inflection screens: detailed breakdowns of 52-week highs and lows, plus screens that catch fundamentals quietly turning (backtested)
Alt-data and retail sentiment: pulls YouTube transcripts and scrapes Seeking Alpha, Reddit, and X comments alongside SEC filings and FMP fundamentals
Agentic pipelines with LLM map-reduce: multi-step Claude orchestration where cheap models fan out to read and a stronger model synthesizes, tuned to keep costs down at volume
Runs unattended: a headless Claude cron job (launchd) rebuilds every screen and emails digests daily. Re-runnable, tested, and pure Python

experimental.name: Concert Radar
experimental.description: Turns my Spotify listening into a personalized NYC concert radar. A Python pipeline reads what I actually play (recent tracks, heavy rotation, saved albums, playlists) and scores every upcoming show against it, then layers in a discovery lane, venue fit, and live price signals. A React site and a weekly email tell me which shows are worth catching.

images.caption: This Week's Top Picks: every upcoming NYC show scored against your Spotify taste

highlights: Builds a taste profile from 12 Spotify signals: recent plays, top artists and tracks across short, medium, and long-term windows, saved albums, followed artists, and playlists
Log-scaled affinity so favorites outrank casual likes, which fixed v1's habit of flattening everyone into one tier
Three lanes: your artists, a Last.fm 'New To You' discovery lane (weighted down so it never crowds out artists you already love), and 'Worth The Trip' destination shows
Ranks every show by taste, venue fit, and live Ticketmaster and SeatGeek price and urgency (selling fast, from $X, in N days)
Filters out tribute bands and makes obscure artists match only on exact lineups, so fuzzy noise never sneaks in
Pure standard-library Python with zero dependencies. Rebuilds daily and emails a weekly digest via launchd

experimental.name: Baton
experimental.description: A macOS menu-bar command center for the AI agents you have running. It reads the signals Claude Code and Codex already emit, so there's nothing to enter by hand, and shows you the one that matters: whichever agent finished its leg and is now waiting on you ("the baton's with you").

images.caption: Menu-bar view: every Claude Code and Codex session, sorted by who's waiting on you

highlights: Event-driven detection via macOS FSEvents catches an agent hand-off in under a second, with no polling in between, so it stays fast and easy on the battery
Unifies two separate agent tools behind one normalized "track" format, so adding a new source never touches the UI
Reads each tool's real waiting signal: Claude Code's live session status and Codex's own unread (blue-dot) state, parsed from its Electron store
Click a session to jump straight to it: it raises the exact Terminal.app tab by tty, or opens the Codex thread through its reverse-engineered codex:// link
Standard-library Python in an isolated venv. Auto-starts at login via a launchd agent, binds to 127.0.0.1, and reads local state only

experimental.name: World Cup 2026 Dashboard
experimental.description: Live dashboard for the 2026 World Cup: scores, group standings, the Golden Boot race, a knockout bracket, and AI-written match recaps. Everything comes live from ESPN's public API, with no backend.

images.caption: Live scores and group standings on the Today tab

highlights: Real traction: 640+ views from 300+ unique devices in its first weeks live (as of Jul 2026)
Live scores, 12-group standings, and the Golden Boot race, all parsed in the browser from ESPN's public API (no backend, no keys)
Qualification-aware: a best-third-place race and a knockout bracket that fills in as teams advance
AI-written match recaps via Claude, with ESPN's own report as a no-key fallback
Auto-refreshes every 60 seconds. Mobile-first, with zero horizontal overflow down to 390px

experimental.name: NYC AI Agent
experimental.description: Autonomous AI agent that researches NYC tech news, events, and trends from multiple sources, then delivers a styled daily intelligence brief to your inbox. Features a 9-tool agent loop, stateful memory, and interactive chat mode.
highlights: 9-tool agent loop using Anthropic's tool_runner with up to 15 autonomous iterations per run
Multi-source retrieval: NewsAPI, HackerNews Algolia, and Luma Events API
Stateful JSON memory that tracks items across runs and learns from user feedback
Generates styled HTML email briefs delivered automatically via Gmail CLI
3 modes: automated daily brief, interactive chat, and feedback loop for preference tuning
Scheduled via launchd for fully hands-off daily execution

experimental.name: FAA Bird Strikes: Predicting Aircraft Damage
experimental.description: End-to-end data science project analyzing 316,823 wildlife-aircraft collisions from 1990-2024. Built 3 ML models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost) to predict aircraft damage, diagnosed and fixed overfitting, and validated with cross-validation.

images.caption: Model diagnostics: train vs test AUC and cross-validation scores

highlights: Models revealed animal size (58%), aircraft mass (11%), and altitude (8%) as the top 3 damage drivers. Lighter GA planes hitting large birds are 10x more vulnerable than commercial jets
Pilot warnings barely reduce damage. Warned and unwarned strikes end up nearly identical, which suggests they happen too fast to do anything about
Strikes hit record highs but the severe-damage rate fell 88% (1.75% to 0.21%), a sign that post-2009 engine certifications and wildlife programs are quietly working
Caught a 20% overfitting gap in Random Forest. Three hyperparameter constraints turned the worst model (77.5% AUC) into the best (87.8% AUC)

experimental.name: Shakespeare Transformer: Training from Scratch
experimental.description: Built a 10.8M parameter transformer from raw PyTorch, the same architecture as GPT and Claude. Trained it on Shakespeare using an H100 GPU, with 7 visualizations that show how the model learns language.

images.caption: Training loss dropping from random guessing to coherent Shakespeare
Model predicts next character with 98.8% confidence

highlights: Loss dropped from 4.3 (random guessing) to 1.07 in 5,000 training steps on an H100 GPU
Attention heads self-organized: early layers learn spelling, deep layers learn grammar
Model predicts 'wherefore art thou R→o' with 98.8% confidence, having learned Shakespeare's patterns
Embedding space reveals the model discovered letter categories (vowels, consonants, punctuation) on its own

experimental.name: AlphaGenome: Eye Color Variant Analysis
experimental.description: Investigated how a single DNA mutation determines blue vs brown eye color using DeepMind's AlphaGenome API. Designed controlled experiments proving the model reads DNA sequence rather than memorizing results.

images.caption: 4 experiments proving the model reads DNA sequence
RNA-seq: blue vs brown eye alleles

highlights: Fed the model shuffled and fake DNA across 4 control tests, and predictions collapsed, proving it reads genetic sequence rather than memorizing known answers
Ran RNA-seq predictions across a 1MB DNA window comparing blue vs brown alleles. OCA2 lit up as the top affected gene, matching decades of lab research
Compared the mutation's effect across different cell types. The signal only fires in melanocytes (pigment cells), confirming it targets the right tissue
Histone mark predictions show the brown-eye allele has a 16x stronger enhancer signal, meaning its 'on switch' for pigment production is far more active

experimental.name: ATC Radar Game
experimental.description: Air Traffic Control radar simulation game. Vector arrivals to runways, sequence departures, and manage waypoints to keep the skies safe.

images.caption: Radar scope with arrivals, departures, and waypoints

highlights: Frame-rate-independent game loop, so physics work the same at 30fps or 120fps
Aircraft with 6 flight states and realistic turn/climb behavior
Enforces real ATC separation rules in both horizontal and vertical dimensions
Animated radar sweep rendered with Canvas gradients

experimental.name: News Tracker
experimental.description: Real-time news aggregator tracking latest articles for Adobe, Coal Energy, and GLP-1s with smart ranking and auto-refresh.

images.caption: Real-time article feed with smart ranking

highlights: Custom ranking algorithm that weighs article freshness and source credibility
Server-side proxy to keep API keys secure and bypass browser restrictions
Boolean search queries (AND/OR) for precise topic matching
15-minute auto-refresh with proper memory cleanup on unmount

experimental.name: New Year's Countdown 2027
experimental.description: Real-time countdown to New Year's Day with year progress tracker and particle visualization system.
highlights: Particle system where elements physically stack on top of each other
Scales from 0 to 4,500 particles proportional to year progress
Layered glass-blur effects with dynamic color-shifting glow
Choreographed entrance animations with staggered timing

experimental.name: Sports Scores
experimental.description: Live sports scores and game tracking across 17 leagues with intelligent ranking.
highlights: Fetches scores from 17 leagues simultaneously using parallel API calls
Ranks games by importance: playoff status, broadcast, team quality, and context
Caps games per league so no single sport dominates the feed
Shows today's games, or falls back to yesterday if nothing's on

experimental.name: Jackson Hole Snow Tracker
experimental.description: Real-time snow conditions dashboard with vintage split-flap display leaderboard and dynamic snowfall visualization.

images.caption: Live snow conditions with split-flap display

highlights: Snowfall animation intensity scales with actual reported snow data
Vintage airport-style split-flap display with 3D flip animations
Scrapes 12 resort websites for live snow data with automatic error recovery
Custom-drawn charts (no charting library) with dynamic axis scaling

roadmap.name: Train a Shakespeare Transformer from Scratch
roadmap.description: 10.8M parameter transformer in raw PyTorch, trained on H100 via Modal

roadmap.name: FAA Bird Strikes: Predicting Aircraft Damage
roadmap.description: 316K wildlife strikes: EDA, 3 classifiers, overfitting diagnosis, and cross-validation. A full DS pipeline from raw data to interactive dashboard.

roadmap.name: Fine-Tune an Open-Source LLM
roadmap.description: LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning on Llama or Mistral with custom data

roadmap.name: Build Backpropagation from Scratch
roadmap.description: Pure NumPy neural net with no autograd, every gradient written by hand

roadmap.name: Mechanistic Interpretability
roadmap.description: Reverse-engineer what specific neurons and circuits learn inside a trained transformer

roadmap.name: RLHF / DPO Alignment
roadmap.description: Train a small model to follow human preferences, the step that turns a predictor into an assistant

roadmap.name: Build a Diffusion Model from Scratch
roadmap.description: Train a denoiser on images, visualize the full denoising process step by step

roadmap.name: NBA Player Value Analysis
roadmap.description: Feature engineering on player stats and salary data to find the most over/underpaid players. EDA, regression, clustering, and visualization storytelling.

roadmap.name: Flight Delay Prediction (BTS On-Time Data)
roadmap.description: Millions of domestic flights. Predict delays using carrier, route, time, and weather features. Full DS pipeline: cleaning, EDA, feature engineering, model comparison.
